dsl_rule.translation_notes: Used sma_100 as the 100-period trend-following indicator. Price move abnormality (>2 std devs) approximated with returns > ±0.02 (2%). Regime shift detection via ADX > 25 indicating strong trend initiation. Exit triggered by trend reversal (price crossing below/above SMA). Signal is bidirectional (both long and short entries based on trend direction reversal).

backtest_rule.reason: Used sma_100 as the 100-period trend-following indicator. Price move abnormality (>2 std devs) approximated with returns > ±0.02 (2%). Regime shift detection via ADX > 25 indicating strong trend initiation. Exit triggered by trend reversal (price crossing below/above SMA). Signal is bidirectional (both long and short entries based on trend direction reversal).

source_rule_text: A trader should use longer-term trend-following indicators to reduce transaction costs and capture abnormally large price moves that occur during major market shifts, rather than relying on shorter-term indicators that generate more frequent signals.
